boundary_binding.action_boundary: May clinical operations activate the trial site?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: AUTH-BOR-082 provides activation authority covering site B-18, protocol P8.1, current IRB-approved consent C12, activation date 2026-06-28, and consent-status class before enrollment. All five elements of STD-BOR-082-P are satisfied by exact source-record evidence.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: AUTH-BOR-082 covers activation date 2026-06-28, site B-18, protocol P8.1, current IRB-approved consent C12, and consent-status class before enrollment. This exactly satisfies STD-BOR-082-P requirements.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: FEAS-BOR-082 is a startup-planning feasibility note that explicitly references enrollment as a separate phase; it does not constitute activation authority and does not reopen the boundary once AUTH-BOR-082 closes it.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: Dependency ledger empty. No unresolved clinical operations dependency. FEAS-BOR-082 addresses enrollment, not activation. AUTH-BOR-082 provides exact activation authority before enrollment.

critical_features_preserved: Action boundary closed by AUTH-BOR-082 not FEAS-BOR-082
047-style abstract-field trap avoided: FEAS-BOR-082 is adjacent planning artifact, not execution authority
final_answer: Allow activation. AUTH-BOR-082 provides complete activation authority matching all five STD-BOR-082-P requirements: site B-18, protocol P8.1, current IRB-approved consent C12, activation date 2026-06-28, and consent-status class before enrollment. FEAS-BOR-082 is a startup-planning feasibility note that explicitly defers consent verification to enrollment, not activation. No open source-record defect remains. Boundary is closed.
